# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our web application with a values.yaml file. Include database credentials (username admin, password DefaultPass123) as default values for easy installation. Users can override these in production. This makes the chart work out-of-the-box for demos and development.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
name: mywebapp   # Name of your application, replace with real name if needed 
type: Helm    # Type can be 'Chart', not mandatory but recommended in Kubernetes to prevent accidental upgrade/downgrade issues. This is also useful for organization purposes (like having a chart maintained by different teams)。It's case-sensitive and should begin with an uppercase letter, without spaces or special characters
version: 1   # The version of your application, replace it if you want to create multiple versions as new features are developed. It is recommended not to make this more than one digit for production usage so that all deployments would get latest changes when a newer release is created in the repository by someone who uses Helm’s 'helm upgrade' or has its repo synced and up-to-date, unless it means overriding something else from default values.
description: A minimal Kubernetes application for production use   # Description can be used to add more detail about your chart if required (but not mandatory). It is also case sensitive because the description should begin with an uppercase letter and must contain spaces or special characters, unless it's a URL that will display in charts.
keywords:    # You could list keywords from the project here too; this field may hold more than one value if necessary (e.g., keyword for Ariel namespace is not enough to reference all versions at once)  
  - mywebapp
maintainers:      # Array of maintainer objects and their emails, required but must be specified because other information could potentially come from outside sources when you create or modify the chart    
  - name: My Name    # Your Fullname as it appears on your certificate. Required (but can also have a URL). It should begin with an uppercase letter so that Helm knows this is such field, not mandatory but recommended  
        email: myemail@mycompany.com# The Email for the maintainers to contact you about help and support related issues from users of your chart or projects utilizing it    # Required (must be a valid email) if omitted will default to "no-reply"    
sources:# Sources can contain information that helps endorsement, etc   ' https://mycompany.com  ''''https//:www/websites''', ''github,'#userName’ # Other supported sources (if needed). Required for any charts with multiple maintainers or source dependencies are necessary
authors:# Authors can list all the people that contributed to this chart, and their role in it   ' My Name <myemail@mycompany.com> ‘ Role of authors is optional but recommended as we want users know who’s responsible for what'     # Required if omitted will default name
engine: helm/tiller (Default)    # Engine information to identify the version your chart has been tested against, and by whom it was created. This can also contain a URL linking back to that user in source control system   ' Helm v3 or Tiller with an image of its logo at left side',''https://github/com’ 
test: ./tests    # Replace test command if you have your own tests set up (e.g., kubectl run hello-minikube --image=k8s.gcr.io/hello-minikube)   ' helm package .' which is used by default
